decks.notes: This deck utilizes a combination of creatures such as Hermit Druid and Gemstone Mine to quickly fill up the graveyard, enabling powerful combos with cards like Karmic Guide and Phyrexian Devourer. The goal is to set up a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent with cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress. The main win condition is to create a loop with cards like Animate Dead or Volrath's Shapeshifter, allowing for a powerful combo finish with Triskelion or Psychatog. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces while controlling the game and overwhelming the opponent with its synergies.

This BW Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of hand disruption spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress, targeted removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate, and board wipes like Wrath of God. The deck also utilizes graveyard recursion with Eternal Dragon and uses card draw and life gain with Skeletal Scrying and Gerrard's Verdict. The objective is to control the game by managing the opponent's threats while establishing card advantage and eventually overpowering them with powerful spells like Decree of Justice and Decree of Pain.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal, and land destruction while generating card advantage with Standstill and Fact or Fiction. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a dominant board position, often with Mishra's Factory as a win condition. This control archetype is focused on disrupting the opponent's strategy and out-valuing them in the long run, leading to a favorable endgame scenario where the deck can secure victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo by using cards like Stifle to counter triggered abilities and manipulate the player's library with cards like Portent and Peek. It also includes counterspells and card draw spells to control the game and find the combo pieces. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Swords to Plowshares and Annul to deal with threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The strategy revolves around efficiently assembling the combo and protecting it until victory.

This aggressive Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures to the battlefield and overwhelming the opponent with constant aggression. By utilizing small creatures like Priest of Gix and Withered Wretch alongside powerful spells like Dark Ritual and Duress, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent while building a board presence. Rotlung Reanimator and Graveborn Muse provide additional value by generating tokens and drawing cards, respectively. The inclusion of powerful reanimation spells like Living Death and Oath of Ghouls allows for explosive plays to close out the game.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with cards like Llanowar Elves and Gaea's Cradle to play powerful creatures such as Masticore and Multani's Acolyte. The deck also utilizes cards like Survival of the Fittest and Wirewood Symbiote to quickly find key cards and protect important creatures. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of elves and other creatures, using cards like Quirion Ranger and Priest of Titania to generate even more mana and keep the pressure on. The deck aims to win by creating a fast and aggressive board presence.

The deck focuses on quick aggressive plays, utilizing cards with the Madness ability to take advantage of discarding cards for value. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with early threats such as Savannah Lions, Wild Mongrel, and Basking Rootwalla, while maintaining board control with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck also includes efficient beaters like Arrogant Wurm and Call of the Herd to close out the game quickly. With a mix of creatures, spells, and lands, it aims to establish a fast and dominant presence on the battlefield.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the madness mechanic to play powerful creatures for a lower cost and maintain aggression against the opponent. The deck includes various cards that enable madness, such as Wild Mongrel and Basking Rootwalla, as well as powerful madness cards like Circular Logic and Arrogant Wurm. By efficiently managing resources and utilizing madness cards effectively, players can overwhelm their opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, while also maintaining card advantage and flexibility in their plays.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with early game aggression by playing efficient creatures and disruption spells. By using cards like Hypnotic Specter and Ravenous Rats to disrupt the opponent's hand and quickly applying pressure with creatures like Withered Wretch and Mishra's Factory, the deck aims to control the game and force the opponent on the defensive. With a focus on resource denial through cards like Dark Ritual and Wasteland, Mono Black Aggro aims to close out games quickly before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

This deck focuses on utilizing enchantments to create a powerful board state and quickly overwhelm the opponent. By ramping up mana with cards like Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum, the deck can quickly cast powerful enchantments like Opalescence and Parallax Wave. The Enchantress creatures and Enchantress's Presence provide card advantage while Sterling Grove and Seal of Cleansing offer protection and removal. The deck aims to win by combining all these elements to create a strong enchantment presence on the battlefield.

The deck focuses on using Ill-Gotten Gains in combination with various cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Lion's Eye Diamond to generate massive amounts of mana and card advantage. It aims to combo off by casting Brain Freeze on the opponent, using cards like Intuition and Cephalid Coliseum to fuel the graveyard with spells. The deck utilizes a mix of card draw, mana acceleration, and disruption spells like Orim's Chant to ensure a smooth and efficient combo kill.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana and control the board with aggressive creatures, while utilizing land destruction with Armageddon to disrupt the opponent's resources. The key strategy is to play Terravore and Werebear early on, taking advantage of the abundance of land cards in the deck. Sylvan Library helps with card advantage, while Seal of Cleansing and Thornscape Battlemage offer additional removal options. The inclusion of Wasteland and Rishadan Port further disrupt the opponent's mana base, giving the deck a strong tempo advantage.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a huge threat while preventing opponents from countering or removing it. The deck also includes card draw and control elements to help find and protect the combo pieces. By efficiently controlling the game and disrupting opponent's plans, the deck aims to win the game through a powerful and hard-to-stop combo.

This deck revolves around quickly filling your graveyard with enchantments using Attunement and Frantic Search, then casting Replenish to bring them all back to the battlefield at once. With cards like Opalescence and Parallax Wave, you can create a powerful board presence and lock down your opponent's creatures. Decree of Silence and Mana Leak provide disruption and protection while you set up your combo. This deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with a barrage of powerful enchantments and control the game until you can combo off for the win.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Stiflenought combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to quickly assemble a powerful threat and disrupt opponent's strategies. The deck contains card draw and countermagic to dig for key pieces and protect the combo while also utilizing cantrips to filter through the deck efficiently. With the ability to quickly deploy a massive threat and disrupt the opponent's game plan, this deck aims to win the game through a powerful and efficient combo strategy in the early turns.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up land destruction and aggressive creatures to overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Armageddon and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base, as well as Terravore to benefit from the land destruction, the deck aims to quickly close out the game with powerful creatures like Nimble Mongoose and Treetop Village. The inclusion of cards like Call of the Herd and Vindicate offer additional versatility and removal options, making it a strong and versatile Aggro - Terrageddon archetype deck in the Premodern 1 vs 1 format.
